title: sorbus aucuparia f. xanthocarpa
intro: Sorbus aucuparia f. xanthocarpa, commonly known as the Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is known for its yellow or red berries and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant's berries have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, colds, and respiratory problems, and also possess antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The berries can be used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be consumed as a tea or added to other remedies to enhance their efficacy.
